title: High-Altitude Balloon Flight Computer (Arduino + LoRa + GPS)
design_question: How to build a lightweight, cold-tolerant flight computer that records and transmits position + environmental data throughout a high-altitude balloon flight?
selected_approach: Arduino Nano (3.3 V, 8 MHz, low power) + u-blox NEO-M8N GPS (airborne dynamic model) + BMP280 pressure/temperature + DS18B20 −55 °C rated external temp probe + SX1276 LoRa 433 MHz radio. All housed in a 100 mm³ polystyrene foam box. Logging to microSD at 1 Hz. LoRa transmits UKHAS sentence ($CALLSIGN,seq,lat,lon,alt,temp,batt*checksum) every 10 s. AA lithium batteries (Energizer Ultimate). Nichrome cutdown on GPIO trigger.
rationale: u-blox NEO-M8N with UBX CFG-NAV5 airborne mode supports altitude to 50 km without firmware lock. LoRa 433 MHz gives 500+ km range at altitude with a simple dipole antenna. Polystyrene box maintains internal temperature > 0 °C during −50 °C external conditions. AA lithium cells operate to −40 °C, unlike alkaline.

alternatives.reason_rejected: APRS provides real-time public tracking via APRS-IS network (no ground station needed) but requires ham radio licence; LoRa chosen for licence-free 433 MHz ISM operation.
4G coverage ends at ~3 km altitude — useless above the troposphere. RPi power consumption too high for battery budget.

verification_notes: FACT: 'High-Altitude Balloon Telemetry — GPS, Pressure, and Radio Downlink' confirms u-blox airborne mode, LoRa range, cold temperature requirements, and UKHAS sentence format.
supporting_fact_titles: High-Altitude Balloon Telemetry — GPS, Pressure, and Radio Downlink
GPS Module — NMEA Sentence Parsing and Fix Accuracy
LoRa / LoRaWAN — LPWAN for Remote Telemetry
